perturbation.original_text: For such time as any employees of Provider are providing the Services to Recipient under this Agreement, (a) such employees will remain employees of Provider and shall not be deemed to be employees of Recipient for any purpose, and (b) Provider shall be solely responsible for the payment and provision of all wages, bonuses and commissions, employee benefits, including severance and worker's compensation, and the withholding and payment of applicable taxes relating to such employment.
perturbation.changed_text: For such time as any employees of Provider are providing the Services to Recipient under this Agreement, (a) such employees may, at Recipient's discretion, be deemed employees of Recipient for specific projects, and (b) Provider shall be responsible for wages; however, Recipient assumes responsibility for severance pay, if applicable, and worker's compensation claims arising directly from Recipient-directed projects.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the employment status and responsibilities. Originally, the provider's employees were explicitly stated to remain employees of the provider and not the recipient. The modified version introduces the possibility of them being deemed employees of the recipient for specific projects. Furthermore, the responsibility for severance and worker's compensation is shifted to the Recipient, creating uncertainty. This modification directly contradicts the initial clear designation of employment and related liabilities. This contradicts the original statement that the employees will only be employees of the Provider and therefore all benefits shall be handled by the provider and that the Recipient is not responsible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For such time as any employees of Provider are providing the Services to Recipient under this Agreement, (a) such employees may, at Recipient's discretion, be deemed employees of Recipient for specific projects, and (b) Provider shall be responsible for wages; however, Recipient assumes responsibility for severance pay, if applicable, and worker's compensation claims arising directly from Recipient-directed projects.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text clearly states that employees of the Provider will *not* be deemed employees of the Recipient and that the Provider is solely responsible for all employment-related liabilities. The changed text allows the Recipient to deem these employees as their own for specific projects and shifts responsibility for severance and worker's compensation to the Recipient. This directly conflicts with the original allocation of employment status and liabilities, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text directly contradicts the original statement and introduces uncertainty and conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Provider may not make any change in the Provider Representatives without the prior consent of the Recipient. Provider Representatives shall be dedicated to solely providing the Services to Recipient and shall not provide any such services or resources to Provider or any other customer of Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: Provider may, at its discretion, change the Provider Representatives without prior consent, provided that Provider notifies Recipient within 30 days of such change. Provider Representatives may provide services to other customers with Recipient's implicit or explicit agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Here we created an in-text contradiction related to changing Provider Representatives, originally it was not allowed without approval and here, it says that Provider may, at its discretion, change the Provider Representatives without prior consent. It also introduces the idea that, Provider Representatives may provide services to other customers with Recipient's implicit or explicit agreement. Which also contradicts the original agreement, that Provider Representatives shall be dedicated to solely providing the Services to Recipient and shall not provide any such services or resources to Provider or any other customer of Provider.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Provider shall use its best efforts to provide for employees or contractors to perform the Services, each of whose names, positions, and respective levels of experience and relevant licenses shall be set out in Exhibit A attached hereto (collectively, the "Provider Representatives"). Provider may, at its discretion, change the Provider Representatives without prior consent, provided that Provider notifies Recipient within 30 days of such change. Provider Representatives may provide services to other customers with Recipient's implicit or explicit agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that the Provider may change representatives without prior consent, whereas the original text required prior consent. Additionally, the changed text allows Provider Representatives to serve other customers with Recipient's agreement, directly contradicting the initial requirement that Provider Representatives be dedicated solely to the Recipient. Since the contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, the changes introduce a contradiction in text, by stating the opposite of what was originally agreed upon.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall own, and Provider hereby irrevocably assigns to the Recipient, all rights, title, and interest in any invention, technique, process, device, discovery, improvement, or know-how, whether patentable or not and all other proprietary rights, industrial rights and any other similar rights, in each case on a worldwide basis, and all copies and tangible embodiments thereof, or any part thereof, in whatever form or medium hereafter made or conceived solely or jointly by Provider while working for or on behalf of the Recipient, which relate to, is suggested by, or results from the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall own a shared title and interest with Provider in any invention, technique, process, device, discovery, improvement, or know-how, whether patentable or not and all other proprietary rights, industrial rights and any other similar rights, in each case on a worldwide basis, and all copies and tangible embodiments thereof, or any part thereof, in whatever form or medium hereafter made or conceived solely or jointly by Provider while working for or on behalf of the Recipient, which relate to, is suggested by, or results from the Services.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, it was stating that Recipient shall own and Provider hereby irrevocably assigns to the Recipient. With the added change, now the text states, Recipient shall own a shared title and interest with Provider. Which contradicts the original statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Recipient shall own a shared title and interest with Provider in any invention, technique, process, device, discovery, improvement, or know-how, whether patentable or not and all other proprietary rights, industrial rights and any other similar rights, in each case on a worldwide basis, and all copies and tangible embodiments thereof, or any part thereof, in whatever form or medium hereafter made or conceived solely or jointly by Provider while working for or on behalf of the Recipient, which relate to, is suggested by, or results from the Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient shall own and Provider hereby irrevocably assigns to the Recipient. The changed text introduces a shared ownership model with the Provider. This directly contradicts the notion of sole ownership by the Recipient established in the original text. The contradicted text also states a shared title and interest, but it only serves to highlight that a change in the original text to also imply shared interest leads to an internal contradiction, since it contradicts the original clause which stated that the Recipient would own and Provider would irrevocably assign the rights.
